description: Machine-readable governance rules for the design system. Used by AI agents and validation tooling to enforce correct component usage, token discipline, and accessibility standards.

principles.title: Token-First, Always
principles.rule: Every visual property must come from a CSS custom property in variables.css. Raw hex values, pixel values, and hardcoded font sizes are forbidden in component code.

principles.title: Semantic Over Presentational
principles.rule: Choose tokens and variants based on semantic meaning, not visual appearance. Use variant='error' because the state is an error, not because red looks good.

principles.title: Component Before Custom
principles.rule: If a design system component covers the use case, use it. Do not build custom equivalents or style raw HTML elements when a component exists.

principles.title: Composition Within Rules
principles.rule: Components have defined parent/child relationships documented in the component registry. Do not compose components outside these rules.

principles.title: Accessibility Is Non-Negotiable
principles.rule: All interactive components must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Do not remove focus rings, skip heading levels, omit labels, or override ARIA attributes.

required.rule: Use semantic color tokens (--color-fg-*, --color-bg-*, --color-border-*, --color-primary-*, --color-accent-*, --color-success-*, --color-warning-*, --color-error-*) for all color values in component code.
Use spacing tokens (--spacing-0 through --spacing-24) for all padding, margin, and gap values. The scale is 4px-based.
Use radius tokens (--radius-none through --radius-full) for all border-radius values.
Use shadow tokens (--shadow-xs through --shadow-2xl) for all box-shadow values. Use --shadow-focus-ring for focus indicators.
Use typography tokens for all font-family, font-size, font-weight, line-height, and letter-spacing values.
Use motion tokens for all transition duration and easing values in animations.
Use state tokens for interactive states. Use --state-disabled-opacity for disabled elements, --state-focus-ring-color for custom focus rings.

forbidden.reason: Primitive color tokens are the source layer. Use semantic tokens instead so components respond to theme changes.
Hardcoded colors bypass the token system and break theming.
Spacing must map to the 4px-based spacing token scale.
Use var(--state-disabled-opacity) so the disabled opacity can be adjusted globally.

do: Use variant='primary' for the single most important action in a view.
Use variant='danger' for destructive actions, always with a Dialog confirmation.
Set loading and disabled together during async operations.
Use variant='ghost' for icon-only or low-priority actions.
dont: Never place more than one variant='primary' Button in the same visual group.
Never use Button for page navigation — use anchor or router Link.
Never remove the button focus ring.
Never hardcode background or text colors.

do: Always associate with a label via FormField or label prop.
Use error prop for validation messages.
Use helperText for format hints.
dont: Never use for multi-line content — use Textarea.
Never use placeholder as a substitute for a label.

do: Choose variant based on semantic meaning, not color preference.
Keep label text to 1–3 words.
dont: Never make Badge interactive.
Never use Badge to replace Alert for page-level messages.

do: Use variant='border' as default.
Use variant='ghost' for nested cards.
Only set hoverable={true} for fully-clickable navigation cards.
dont: Never nest Card/border inside Card/border.
Never use Card as a full-page layout wrapper — use Container.

do: Use color='muted' for secondary copy.
Use color='subtle' for captions and metadata.
dont: Never use Text for headings — use Heading.
Never apply inline font-size or color styles.

do: Always set level to match document hierarchy.
Never skip heading levels.
dont: Never use more than one h1 per page.
Never use Heading for body copy.

do: Use for selecting any number of options (including none).
Use indeterminate for partial group selection.
dont: Never use for a binary toggle — use Switch.
Never use for single-exclusive selection — use Radio.

do: Always wrap in RadioGroup.
Use for 2–6 mutually exclusive options.
dont: Never manage radio state manually outside RadioGroup.
Never use for > 6 options — use Select.

do: Use for 5+ options.
Always provide a placeholder for required fields.
dont: Never use for fewer than 3 options — use Radio.
Never render <option> children manually.

do: Use for settings that apply immediately.
dont: Never use inside a form that requires explicit submission — use Checkbox.

do: Wrap every form control in FormField.
Pass error from form validation state.
dont: Never build custom label/error layouts outside FormField.
Never pass both helperText and error simultaneously.

do: Use for destructive confirmations, focused forms, and detail views.
Always include DialogTitle.
Place cancel left, confirm right in DialogFooter.
dont: Never use for non-blocking notifications — use Toast.
Never stack multiple Dialogs.
Never auto-open without user intent.

do: Use for icon buttons where the label is not always visible.
Keep content under 10 words.
dont: Never put interactive elements inside tooltip content.
Never use as the only means of conveying critical information.

do: Use for persistent, in-context status messages.
Always match variant to semantic meaning.
dont: Never use for transient messages — use Toast.
Never use for per-field validation — use error prop on control.

do: Use for transient confirmations after user actions.
Always trigger via useToast hook.
dont: Never use for destructive confirmations — use Dialog.
Never stack more than 3 toasts.
Never use for persistent status — use Alert.

do: Use for secondary navigation within a page.
Always provide a default active tab.
dont: Never use for primary site navigation.
Never exceed 7 tab items.

do: Use as the primary flex layout primitive.
Control spacing via gap — never add margin to individual children.
dont: Never add margin/padding to Stack children for spacing.
Never hardcode gap values.

do: Use as the outermost page content wrapper.
Use as='main' for primary content.
dont: Never nest Container inside Container.
Never set custom max-width values.

do: Always provide alt text when src is set.
Always provide name for initials fallback.
dont: Never render more than 3 standalone Avatars side by side — use AvatarGroup.
Never apply custom border-radius.

do: Use for 3 or more avatars in compact contexts.
Set size on AvatarGroup, not individual Avatar children.
dont: Never mix sizes across Avatar children.
Never use for fewer than 3 avatars.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Using inline styles to set values that should come from design tokens
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Use component props (variant, size, padding) or CSS custom properties via className.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Hardcoded Tailwind color classes that bypass the semantic token system
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Use semantic component variants: <Alert variant='error'>, <Text color='accent'>.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Custom-styled <button> element instead of Button component
Removing focus rings from interactive elements
forbiddenPatterns.pattern: outline: none | outline: 0 | focus:outline-none without replacement
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Never remove focus rings. The design system provides --shadow-focus-ring.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Skipping heading levels breaks screen reader navigation
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Use Heading level prop sequentially. Size and level are independent props.

forbiddenPatterns.description: More than one primary Button in the same visual group
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Demote secondary actions to variant='secondary' or variant='ghost'.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Using Toast for destructive action confirmations
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Use Dialog with DialogTitle, description, and cancel/confirm Button pair.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Using --color-primitive-* tokens directly in component code
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Use the corresponding semantic token: var(--color-accent-default).

forbiddenPatterns.description: Avatar with src but no alt attribute
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Always provide alt matching the person's full name.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Icon-only Button without an accessible label
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Add aria-label='Close' (or descriptive label) to the Button.
Always include <DialogTitle> inside <DialogHeader>. May be visually hidden but must be present.
Use nested Stack for inner layout instead of nested Containers.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Adding margin to Stack children for spacing instead of using gap
forbiddenPatterns.pattern: Direct Stack children with margin-top or margin-bottom classes
forbiddenPatterns.remedy: Set gap on Stack. Remove margin from children.

requiredChildren: one of: Input | Select | Textarea | Checkbox | Switch | RadioGroup
compositionRules.notes: FormField manages label, helper text, and error propagation for its child control.
DialogContent must contain DialogTitle for accessibility.
DialogTitle may be visually hidden but must be present in the DOM.
Place cancel/secondary action on the left, confirm/primary action on the right.
TabsList must contain TabsTrigger items. Each TabsTrigger value must match a TabsContent value.
Set size on AvatarGroup, not on individual Avatar children.
RadioGroup manages shared name, value, onChange, and disabled state for all child Radio items.
The last BreadcrumbItem must have current={true} and no href.

compositionRules.forbiddenNesting: Card variant='border' or variant='shadow' inside another Card with the same variant

requirements.rule: Normal text (< 18pt) must have a minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio against its background.
Large text (≥ 18pt or bold ≥ 14pt) must have a minimum 3:1 contrast ratio.
Never communicate meaning with color alone — always pair with a text label, icon, or pattern.
All interactive elements must show a visible focus indicator. Use --shadow-focus-ring. Never set outline: none without an equivalent replacement.
Focus order must follow a logical reading sequence. Do not use positive tabIndex values.
When a Dialog opens, focus moves to the first interactive element or dialog title. When it closes, focus returns to the trigger.
Every form control must have an associated visible label. FormField handles this automatically. Use aria-label only as a last resort.
Error messages must be programmatically associated with their control via aria-describedby. FormField handles this automatically.
Do not skip heading levels. Use Heading level prop to maintain a logical document outline.
Informative images require descriptive alt text. Decorative images require alt=''.
Icon-only buttons must have aria-label or aria-labelledby providing a text alternative.
All animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion. Framer Motion components must include a reducedMotion variant.

componentDecisionMatrix.notes: Always confirm destructive actions with a Dialog before executing.
Stays in the layout until dismissed or resolved.

componentDecisionMatrix.useCase: Contextual rich content anchored to trigger
Short label for icon or control
componentDecisionMatrix.notes: Content must be non-interactive and under 10 words.

componentDecisionMatrix.useCase: Status or category label on entity
componentDecisionMatrix.notes: Choose variant based on semantic meaning.
Always provide name prop for fallback.
Only when gap is insufficient to convey the break.
